    Michelle Zhao

    PhD Candidate at CMU

    Teaching

    I am committed to teaching and mentorship! My teaching is informed by training from the CMU Eberly Center for Teaching Excellence and Educational Innovation, where I developed skills in inclusive classroom practices, effective lecture delivery, constructive feedback, and accessible course design.

    During my time at Carnegie Mellon University, I was a co-instructor with Zackory Erickson for Introduction to Human-Robot Interaction: Foundations (Spring 2025), where I was involved in course delivery, curriculum development, and lectures. In addition, I was also a teaching assistant for undergraduate and graduate courses on Human-Robot Interaction (Fall 2022, Spring 2022). Prior to that, at Caltech, I served as a teaching assistant for courses spanning machine learning and introductory programming (2017–2020).

    Co-Instructor

    Introduction to Human Robot Interaction - Foundations, Spring 2025 (Course Link)

    Teaching Assistant (TA)

    Teaching Assistant, Graduate Human Robot Interaction, CMU, Fall 2022
    Teaching Assistant, Undergraduate Human Robot Interaction, CMU, Spring 2022
    Teaching Assistant, Networks: Structure and Economics, Caltech, Winter 2020
    Teaching Assistant, Machine Learning and Data Mining, Caltech, Winter 2019
    Teaching Assistant, Machine Learning Systems, Caltech, Fall 2018
    Teaching Assistant, Java Computer Programming Lab, Caltech, Fall 2017
